title: Amy Madigan's Strong Oscar Prospects Following SAG Win
text: Amy Madigan's performance as the terrifying Aunt Gladys in the horror film Weapons has positioned her as a frontrunner for the Best Supporting Actress Oscar. The 75-year-old actress secured the Screen Actors Guild Award, which statistically suggests Oscar victory. Key Stats: Since 2009, the SAG Award winner in this category has gone on to win the Academy Award in 16 of 17 cases. The sole exception occurred in 2018 when Emily Blunt won at SAG despite Regina King ultimately claiming the Oscar for If Beale Street Could Talk. Recognition Across Awards: Madigan has accumulated impressive accolades this season: Won Critics Choice, New York Film Critics Circle, and Online Film Critics Society awards, Received Golden Globe and Saturn Award nominations, Earned over 60 total award recognitions on IMDb. Director Zach Cregger expressed enthusiasm about her achievements: I'm so happy for her. Whether she wins or doesn't is kind of immaterial to me. I'm just thrilled that she's been recognized. Historic Achievement: Madigan's nomination represents a significant milestone, as horror performances rarely receive acting recognition from the Academy. Previous winners in this genre remain exceptionally rare, making her nomination particularly noteworthy. The 2026 Academy Awards ceremony takes place March 15th.
